---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 7c1324cedf62685e9ca021a222b42a188ee736a8
findings:
  - { id: judged-proposal-never-illustrative-vs-parent-three-valued-coverage, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 concludes 'a proposal is always full or partial, never illustrative' (routing every class: proposal artifact to the extractor, whose result vocabulary is full/partial only), but parent dc-3 defines verification coverage as three-valued PER ARTIFACT with illustrative meaning 'no generator' — a coverage tier, not a class. Under the parent's definition, a class: proposal diagram whose kind has no generator (e.g. a sequence-kind proposal) lands in the illustrative coverage tier; under the story's composed sibling reading (illustrative-class dc-2: illustrative is decided BY CLASS before any extractor call), that artifact has no representable coverage outcome at all — it can neither be illustrative (wrong class) nor honestly full/partial (nothing to extract). This also strains parent dc-1's 'failing closed to a lower verification tier': the lowest tier available to a proposal becomes partial, not illustrative, silently removing the parent's fail-closed floor for proposals. The story grounds the move in already-ratified sibling decisions but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3 (or dc-1), so the reinterpretation of illustrative from coverage-tier to class is undeclared with respect to the parent. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-frontmatter-enumeration-drops-base-digest-from-dc7-batch, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 enumerates 'the ratified 02 diagram-artifact FRONTMATTER FIELDS (id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from — what the artifact itself decodes)' and argues only three of parent dc-7's four batch items are frontmatter fields, with the illustrative marker as the non-field fourth. But parent dc-7's second batch item is 'derived_from PLUS base-digest' — two ratified schema elements counted as one. The story's enumeration silently omits base-digest even though the story's own dc-2 depends on it ('stale-base digest comparison'). If base-digest is a distinct decoded frontmatter field ratified by dc-7, the story's claimed-complete enumeration contradicts the parent's ratified schema surface without any declared edge; if it is instead nested inside derived_from, the story should say so rather than presenting an enumeration that reads as exhaustive while dropping a ratified element. (confidence 0.45)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/alignment-section#dc-1, spec/alignment-section#dc-2, spec/alignment-section#dc-3, spec/alignment-section#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"proposal-never-illustrative-vs-parent-three-valued-coverage\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 concludes 'a proposal is always full or partial, never illustrative' (routing every class: proposal artifact to the extractor, whose result vocabulary is full/partial only), but parent dc-3 defines verification coverage as three-valued PER ARTIFACT with illustrative meaning 'no generator' — a coverage tier, not a class. Under the parent's definition, a class: proposal diagram whose kind has no generator (e.g. a sequence-kind proposal) lands in the illustrative coverage tier; under the story's composed sibling reading (illustrative-class dc-2: illustrative is decided BY CLASS before any extractor call), that artifact has no representable coverage outcome at all — it can neither be illustrative (wrong class) nor honestly full/partial (nothing to extract). This also strains parent dc-1's 'failing closed to a lower verification tier': the lowest tier available to a proposal becomes partial, not illustrative, silently removing the parent's fail-closed floor for proposals. The story grounds the move in already-ratified sibling decisions but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3 (or dc-1), so the reinterpretation of illustrative from coverage-tier to class is undeclared with respect to the parent.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"frontmatter-enumeration-drops-base-digest-from-dc7-batch\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 enumerates 'the ratified 02 diagram-artifact FRONTMATTER FIELDS (id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from — what the artifact itself decodes)' and argues only three of parent dc-7's four batch items are frontmatter fields, with the illustrative marker as the non-field fourth. But parent dc-7's second batch item is 'derived_from PLUS base-digest' — two ratified schema elements counted as one. The story's enumeration silently omits base-digest even though the story's own dc-2 depends on it ('stale-base digest comparison'). If base-digest is a distinct decoded frontmatter field ratified by dc-7, the story's claimed-complete enumeration contradicts the parent's ratified schema surface without any declared edge; if it is instead nested inside derived_from, the story should say so rather than presenting an enumeration that reads as exhaustive while dropping a ratified element.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}\n```" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-proposal-never-illustrative-vs-parent-three-valued-coverage** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 concludes 'a proposal is always full or partial, never illustrative' (routing every class: proposal artifact to the extractor, whose result vocabulary is full/partial only), but parent dc-3 defines verification coverage as three-valued PER ARTIFACT with illustrative meaning 'no generator' — a coverage tier, not a class. Under the parent's definition, a class: proposal diagram whose kind has no generator (e.g. a sequence-kind proposal) lands in the illustrative coverage tier; under the story's composed sibling reading (illustrative-class dc-2: illustrative is decided BY CLASS before any extractor call), that artifact has no representable coverage outcome at all — it can neither be illustrative (wrong class) nor honestly full/partial (nothing to extract). This also strains parent dc-1's 'failing closed to a lower verification tier': the lowest tier available to a proposal becomes partial, not illustrative, silently removing the parent's fail-closed floor for proposals. The story grounds the move in already-ratified sibling decisions but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3 (or dc-1), so the reinterpretation of illustrative from coverage-tier to class is undeclared with respect to the parent. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-frontmatter-enumeration-drops-base-digest-from-dc7-batch** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 enumerates 'the ratified 02 diagram-artifact FRONTMATTER FIELDS (id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from — what the artifact itself decodes)' and argues only three of parent dc-7's four batch items are frontmatter fields, with the illustrative marker as the non-field fourth. But parent dc-7's second batch item is 'derived_from PLUS base-digest' — two ratified schema elements counted as one. The story's enumeration silently omits base-digest even though the story's own dc-2 depends on it ('stale-base digest comparison'). If base-digest is a distinct decoded frontmatter field ratified by dc-7, the story's claimed-complete enumeration contradicts the parent's ratified schema surface without any declared edge; if it is instead nested inside derived_from, the story should say so rather than presenting an enumeration that reads as exhaustive while dropping a ratified element. (confidence 0.45)
